articles.title: The RAM shortage could last years
articles.summary: RAM shortage expected to persist until 2027-2030 as suppliers can only meet 60% of demand despite ramping production. Major chipmakers Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are working to increase capacity.

articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: Bill Peebles, leader of OpenAI's Sora video generation tool, is leaving the company after OpenAI discontinued the project. The departure reflects OpenAI's strategic shift toward coding and enterprise focus.

articles.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
articles.summary: Tinder now lets users verify their identity through World's iris-scanning orbs to receive five free boosts. Sam Altman's identity verification service is expanding to select markets including the US and Japan.

articles.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
articles.summary: Tesla has expanded its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston, joining Austin as the third Texas city. The company now offers fully autonomous rides without safety drivers following its January 2026 launch.

articles.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
articles.summary: AI chip startup Cerebras filed for IPO following major deals with AWS and OpenAI valued over $10 billion, positioning itself as a key player in AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
articles.summary: Despite Pentagon designation as a supply-chain risk, Anthropic maintains dialogue with Trump administration officials, suggesting an improving relationship.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100M in Series B funding to challenge AWS with AI-native infrastructure, addressing limitations of legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding features, but open-source alternative Goose offers identical functionality for free, running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco. The startup used AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge to recruit engineers, with 430 participants solving the puzzle.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: US-sanctioned cryptocurrency exchange Grinex halted operations after a $15 million heist, attributing the attack to state-sponsored hackers from 'unfriendly states' targeting Russian users.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: The Flame malware exploited MD5 cryptographic weaknesses to forge Microsoft certificates and distribute malicious updates. Recent advances in quantum computing threaten current encryption standards, pushing critical infrastructure toward vulnerability.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers due to dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. CEO Rajiv Ramaswami stated the company has about 30,000 total customers, with many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Pie Day 2026
articles.summary: Ellie organized a Pi Day 2026 celebration at MIT's food institute, coordinating the baking of 30 pies. Behind-the-scenes details showcase the effort involved in this special mathematical celebration.

articles.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
articles.summary: French geneticists challenge the popular theory of Neanderthal-human interbreeding, suggesting population structure rather than breeding explains genetic similarities in modern humans.

articles.title: The case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Stewart Brand's new book 'Maintenance' explores how repair and upkeep of tools and systems profoundly impact civilization. The work argues that maintaining things—from motorcycles to monuments—is a radical act deserving honor.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual OCR model using synthetic data, enabling efficient text recognition across multiple languages without expensive human-annotated datasets.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Ecom-RLVE introduces adaptive verifiable environments for e-commerce conversational agents, enabling AI systems to interact more effectively in online shopping scenarios with improved verification and learning capabilities.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: The article discusses how AI systems can autonomously generate and submit pull requests, automating code contributions that developers would typically create themselves.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impact. The conversation explores emerging trends and developments shaping the next phase of artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. These solutions enable companies to streamline workflows and produce more imaginative marketing materials.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming technology. The initiative aims to help farmers adopt AI and advanced technologies for sustainable agriculture.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google's latest tools help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations more efficiently this summer. These smart features streamline the travel planning process from booking to exploration.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's AI Mode upgrades transform web interaction with new AI-powered features. The enhancement improves how users engage with online content through artificial intelligence.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your personal context and Google Photos data, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control over AI-generated speech for more expressive audio generation.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robotics through improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding, enabling robots to perform complex real-world tasks more effectively.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, the most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The episode discusses these releases alongside updates on Mamba 3 and attention residuals in recent AI developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that uses generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and Cursor's coding automations. ChatGPT and Anthropic added interactive visualization features.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces sandbox execution in its Agents SDK, enabling enterprise teams to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and better governance. The new infrastructure offers model-native capabilities and improved visibility for moving systems from prototype to production.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems announced AI partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud to combine AI with physics-based simulation for robotic systems and semiconductor design. The collaboration integrates Cadence's simulation tools with Nvidia's CUDA-X libraries and Omniverse platform.

articles.title: Commvault launches a ‘Ctrl-Z’ for cloud AI workloads
articles.summary: Commvault launched AI Protect, an undo feature for cloud AI workloads that monitors and rolls back autonomous AI agent actions across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud to prevent unintended consequences.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture that improves autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data and updating their own weights through learned self-editing via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster responses reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems surrounding them are emerging as the real competitive advantage for AI companies.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets macOS systems running OpenClaw AI agents, exploiting binaries to steal credentials from developers deploying local AI agents on Mac Minis.

articles.title: NVIDIA Releases Ising: the First Open Quantum AI Model Family for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Ising, the first open quantum AI model family designed to help researchers and enterprises build quantum processors for real-world applications by improving calibration and reducing error accumulation from environmental noise.

articles.title: xAI Launches Standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs, Targeting Enterprise Voice Developers
articles.summary: xAI launched Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs for enterprise developers, competing with ElevenLabs and Deepgram. The APIs support 25 languages and are built on infrastructure powering Grok Voice across mobile, Tesla, and Starlink.

articles.title: A Coding Tutorial for Running PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA with GGUF, Benchmarking, Chat, JSON, and RAG
articles.summary: Tutorial on running PrismML's Bonsai 1-bit LLM on CUDA using GGUF format, covering setup, benchmarking, chat, JSON generation, and RAG workflows for efficient lightweight model deployment.

articles.title: How I share audio from my Android phone to multiple earbuds (and why it's a big deal)
articles.summary: Android's audio sharing feature enables two people to listen simultaneously on separate earbuds from one device, provided both support the technology.

articles.title: Why I'm recommending last year's phones over 2026 models - with one exception
articles.summary: Last year's flagship phones like Google Pixel 10 and Samsung Galaxy S25 Plus offer better value than 2026 models, with one notable exception. Older devices remain appealing despite newer releases.

articles.title: This powerful Gemini setting made my AI results way more personal and accurate
articles.summary: Enabling Personal Intelligence in Gemini and connecting Google apps allows the AI to anticipate user needs without explicit requests, delivering more personalized and accurate results.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching in LLMs reduces costs and latency when calling APIs at scale by storing and reusing previous computations for repeated inputs.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article discusses using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering to improve code organization and efficiency.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems handling long documents. Methods improve efficiency and accuracy when processing extensive contextual information.

articles.title: Dreaming in Cubes
articles.summary: Researchers use VQ-VAE and Transformers to generate Minecraft worlds, combining vector quantization with deep learning to create procedural game environments.

articles.title: KV Cache Is Eating Your VRAM. Here’s How Google Fixed It With TurboQuant.
articles.summary: Google's TurboQuant is a KV cache quantization framework using multi-stage compression to reduce VRAM usage while maintaining performance. It enables larger context windows with minimal memory overhead through PolarQuant and QJL residual techniques.

articles.title: Your RAG System Retrieves the Right Data — But Still Produces Wrong Answers. Here’s Why (and How to Fix It).
articles.summary: RAG systems can retrieve correct documents but produce wrong answers when conflicting information exists in the same retrieval window. A lightweight pipeline layer fix resolves this without additional models or GPU resources.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW the same day as its worldwide release, allowing cloud streaming to any device. Five additional titles join the platform this week.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories prioritizing inference workloads. Enterprises should shift from traditional metrics like FLOPS per dollar to cost per token as the primary TCO assessment metric.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe is unveiling a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology to enhance video editing workflows for professional creators.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically tracking costs by IAM principal and enabling detailed cost allocation across teams and projects through AWS billing tools.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. The approach uses intelligent routing to reduce end-to-end search time from 2-4 seconds.

articles.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enables efficient video semantic search, allowing organizations to quickly find specific moments across video content for sports highlights, archival searches, and news retrieval.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, allowing AI coding agents to generate production code at scale. Companies like Stripe and Ramp report significant productivity gains with agents authoring thousands of pull requests weekly.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable secure, always-on local AI agents that evolve beyond Q&A systems to autonomous assistants capable of reading files, calling APIs, and executing multi-step workflows.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI and physics are being used to accelerate the design of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear reactors that are safe, clean, and economical.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with unconventional background developed innovative XR technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in extended reality.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making processes for safer AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven methods for designing imaging systems that optimize how well noisy measurements distinguish objects. The approach uses encoders and noise models to quantify measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
